resources.string: Can\'t change brightness in live view
Can\'t change contrast in live view
Can\'t change color filter in live view
Can\'t change horizontal scroll in live view
Can\'t change vertical scroll in live view

The Open Magnifier is an immersive, privacy-first, open-source image magnifier designed to work seamlessly across various hardware. User can flip between live view (camera) and freeze frame (image)

Quick Tutorial

* Toggle between live and freeze frame: Single tap the screen or press Space on the keyboard
* Adjust zoom: Pinch the screen with two finger or press Z on the keyboard
* Scroll (freeze frame): Swipe the screen or press the arrow keys on the keyboard
* Display help: Long press the screen or press H on the keyboard

Touchscreen Gestures

* Single tap toggles between the live view and freeze frame
* Pinching the screen with two fingers zooms in or out (live view and freeze frame)
* Double tap opens the preference dialog (live view only)
* Long press opens the help dialog (live view only)

Keyboard Controls

* Space bar toggles between (live view and freeze frame)
* z changes zoom levels (live view and freeze frame)

* Arrow keys scrolls image (freeze frame only)
* b changes brightness (freeze frame only)
* c changes contrast (freeze frame only)
* f changes color filters (freeze frame only)
* h shows help
* r changes rotation (freeze frame only)
* x changes left/right scroll distance (freeze frame only)
* y changes up/down scroll distance (freeze frame only)
* v shows version number

About

Open Magnifier Copyright © 2026 Mark Tamura

This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

Open Source Libraries
